channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Bizarre Facebook scam falsely offers Aldi “meat boxes” for under $10 — but just steals your card details
item.description: If you're getting a box of meat for a price of a sandwich, it's probably a scam, researchers explain.

item.title: Can OpenAI’s ‘Master of Disaster’ Fix AI’s Reputation Crisis?
item.description: Global affairs chief Chris Lehane wants to tone down the debate over AI’s societal impacts—and get states to pass laws that won’t derail OpenAI’s meteoric rise.

item.title: I've looked through Samsung's Memorial Day sale — it includes OLED TVs from $1,099, a record-low Galaxy S26 Ultra deal, and $1,700 off appliances
item.description: Samsung's Memorial Day deals already include Black Friday-beating price cuts on the latest TVs, appliances, and the Galaxy S26 series.

item.title: Alibaba's proprietary Qwen3.7-Max can run for 35 hours autonomously and supports external harnesses like Anthropic's Claude Code
item.description: The AI industry has fully entered the "agent era," a paradigm where AI models do far more than generate text — they now actively plan, execute, and course-correct complex tasks over days rather than seconds. Thus, it's perhaps unsurprising to see Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba's famed Qwen Team of AI researchers release a model capable of performing autonomous agentic AI work over multiple days: that model has arrived in the form of Qwen3.7-Max which the company reports in a blog post achieved "~35 hours of continuous autonomous execution" — albeit, in a proprietary, not open source format, ...

item.title: Anker’s new earbuds are the first with its AI chip that boosts noise reduction
item.description: Anker has announced a new version of its Soundcore Liberty Pro earbuds: the new Liberty 5 Pro. These are the company's first earbuds featuring the Thus AI audio chip Anker announced last month, here being used to bolster noise reduction capabilities and help ensure the user's voice can be clearly heard during calls, even in […]

item.title: Anker’s new earbuds have the best call quality I’ve ever heard
item.description: Soundcore, Anker's audio brand, has mostly lived in the budget-to-midrange world, but with its new Liberty 5 Pro earbuds, it's aiming at the big guys. The two new earbuds - the Liberty 5 Pro and Liberty 5 Pro Max - use Anker's new Thus chip, which has more processing power than previous Soundcore earbuds to […]

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Friday, May 22 (game #810)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Friday, May 22 (game #1076)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Friday, May 22 (game #1579)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: Anker debuts Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro earbuds with its Thus AI chip
item.description: Anker's first products with its Thus AI chip are two powerful sets of earbuds, including one that has an AI note taker.

item.title: Trump cancels AI executive order over concerns of slowing U.S. tech innovation
item.description: Trump’s ambitions for AI have collided with the fears of voters over the impact of the technology on life. President Donald Trump called off plans to sign a new executive order on artificial intelligence hours before an expected White House ceremony Thursday because he said he was worried the measure could dull America’s edge on AI technology.

item.title: You can now add ChatGPT to PowerPoint
item.description: Most users can start generating slides with AI today.

item.title: States ask judge to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster
item.description: A federal judge is officially being tasked with deciding whether to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster. More than 30 states are asking Judge Arun Subramanian to order a sale of the ticketing giant, a "sufficient number" of large amphitheaters, and limit its ability to tie access to its remaining amps to the use of its promotions […]
